sanskrit:
यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम्।
यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम्॥
transliteration: yad āditya-gataṃ tejo jagad bhāsayate ’khilam | yac candramasi yac cāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam ||
literal_meaning: That splendor residing in the sun which illuminates the entire world, and that which is in the moon and in fire—know that brilliance to be Mine.
contextual_meaning: Krishna invites Arjuna to re-attribute the awe he feels toward natural light-sources back to its inner source—his own higher Self—shifting the locus of control from external objects to an internal, stable center.
psychological_state: Awe-catalyzed self-transcendence; moving from external to internal locus of control.
trigger_scenario: Standing before a sunrise on a solo hike, suddenly feeling both infinitely small and mysteriously connected to something vast.
anchor_text: See the world's light as your own inner brilliance.
